core_mechanism: estimation of the branching probabilities of a metastable intermediate state that decays through competing channels, inferred from small-sample counting statistics over individually-resolved discrete stochastic events (multinomial branching-ratio inference)
brief_reason: The abstract extracts a channel branching ratio (>5 between two decay products) and an overall decay probability of intermediate complexes from only ~250 and ~65 individually-counted single reaction events, i.e. estimating the outcome probabilities of a competing-channel branching decay from a small sample of discrete events.
